feedback.details: Sidebar is correctly 260px wide (flex-shrink: 0) with background #111827; root .app-layout has background #0a0a0f and overflow:hidden preventing horizontal overflow. At <768px, sidebar uses position:fixed with transform:translateX(-260px) to hide it, and a hamburger button appears. The main-area has min-width:0 preventing overflow. Layout is solid.

The '/' route renders LandingPage which renders AppLayout with conversationId=undefined. ChatArea shows EmptyState component ('Ask anything about the video library') with suggestion chips. No blank screen, no redirect—valid landing state as per spec. No console errors expected (routing is clean).

useMessages hook fetches via getConversation(id), sets error state on failure. ChatArea shows ErrorState component on error with 'Failed to load messages' text and retry button. Tested: GET /api/conversations/invalid-id returns 404, frontend shows error state. Valid conversations load messages correctly.

CRITICAL BUG: The GET /api/conversations endpoint does NOT return the 'preview' field. Confirmed by live API test: response keys are only ['id', 'title', 'created_at', 'updated_at']. The repository.list_conversations() SQL query includes `(SELECT content FROM messages WHERE conversation_id = c.id ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1) AS preview` but dict(aiosqlite.Row) does not include aliased/computed columns in the resulting dict. Verified with live conversations that have messages (e.g., 'Quick test' has 2 messages but no preview returned). The Sidebar component's ConvItem only renders the preview div when `conv.preview` is truthy, so no previews are ever shown. Title and relative timestamps display correctly; empty state 'No conversations yet' works. Two of three required fields work; preview is completely absent.

Sidebar renders 4x SkeletonRow components during loading state (useConversations sets loading=true initially). Each SkeletonRow has 3 divs with className='skeleton' (shimmer animation via @keyframes shimmer in globals.css). No raw spinner used. Meets the ≥3 animated shimmer rows requirement.

ConvItem applies borderLeft: '3px solid #3b82f6' and background: '#1e293b' when isActive===true (compared via conv.id === activeConversationId). Inactive items have transparent background (or hover state #1e293b with 0.6 opacity). Navigating to a new conversation updates activeConversationId immediately via props. Solid implementation.

Hovering a ConvItem sets hovered state and shows delete button (trash icon). Clicking calls onDeleteRequest which sets confirmId state, rendering ConfirmDialog modal. Confirming calls deleteConversation(id), which is a raw fetch to DELETE /api/conversations/{id}. On success (res.ok or 204) item removed via refetch(). On failure, deleteError=true shows 'Failed to delete. Please try again.' in the dialog. Item stays in list on failure. Full flow is correct. Tested DELETE API returns 204 on success, 404 on invalid ID.

Full-width blue (#3b82f6) button pinned at sidebar top with 'New Chat' label. Clicking calls createConversation() (POST /api/conversations). On success, calls refetch() then navigate('/c/${conv.id}')—route changes to new conversation. On failure, sets newChatError='Could not create conversation. Please try again.' shown below button. Route does not change on failure. Tested: POST /api/conversations with empty body returns new conversation object with id. Correct.

User messages: justifyContent:'flex-end', background:'#2563eb', color:'#f1f5f9', maxWidth:'70%'. Assistant messages: justifyContent:'flex-start', background:'#1e293b', color:'#f1f5f9', maxWidth:'80%'. Corners are '18px 18px 4px 18px' (user) and '18px 18px 18px 4px' (assistant)—these are per-corner values (18px ≈ rounded-2xl = 16px), visually equivalent. Spec says 'rounded-2xl' which is technically 1rem/16px; implementation uses 18px, minor deviation but visually correct.

SkeletonMessages component renders 4 rows with alternating justifyContent values: [flex-end, flex-start, flex-end, flex-start]. Each row contains multiple skeleton div lines with className='skeleton' (shimmer animation). No spinner used. Shown when loading===true and conversationId is set. Meets ≥3 alternating animated rows.

MarkdownRenderer uses ReactMarkdown with remarkGfm plugin, handling bold, italic, bullet/numbered lists, inline code, blockquotes, and tables via GFM. The assistant-content CSS class provides proper styling for all markdown elements. Custom code component handles inline vs block code. ReactMarkdown will render formatted output, not raw syntax characters.

CodeBlock component uses Prism SyntaxHighlighter with oneDark theme and customStyle background #0d1117. Header div shows language label (code-lang-label class) and CopyButton. Language defaults to 'plaintext' when null (null || 'plaintext'). When no language, renders plain pre/code with same #0d1117 background. Copy button shows 'Copied!' feedback. Matches all spec requirements.

autoScrollRef (useRef<boolean>) tracks whether auto-scroll is active. handleScroll calculates distFromBottom = scrollHeight - scrollTop - clientHeight and sets autoScrollRef.current = distFromBottom < 100. useEffect triggers scrollToBottom when messages.length or streamingContent changes, but only if autoScrollRef.current===true. After loading completes, snaps to bottom with 50ms setTimeout. Correct 100px threshold logic.

adjustHeight sets height='auto' then Math.min(el.scrollHeight, 144)+'px'. maxH=144 which is 6×24px (lineHeight is 24px). overflowY set to 'auto' when scrollHeight>maxH, 'hidden' otherwise. On send, textarea value cleared and height reset to 'auto'. Starts at rows=1 with minHeight:24. Correct implementation.

box-shadow '0 0 0 2px #3b82f6' is applied via inline style to the textarea element (line 91 of ChatInput.tsx) when focused===true. The focused state toggles via onFocus/onBlur handlers. The textarea has outline:'none' inline. The glow is absent when not focused (boxShadow:'none'). Functionally correct. Note: the CSS class .chat-textarea:focus and .chat-input-wrapper:focus-within in globals.css are dead code (no className applied to these elements), but the React state-based implementation works. The spec's exact requirement ('box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #3b82f6 on the textarea element') is met via inline style.

A gradient div is positioned absolute at bottom:0 with height:120px, background:'linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent, #0a0a0f)', pointerEvents:'none', zIndex:1. The chat input is positioned absolute at zIndex:2 above it. The scroll container has paddingBottom:140px to prevent last message from hiding under the input/gradient. Gradient is always rendered (not conditional on scroll position), so it's visible whenever there are enough messages.

handleKeyDown: e.key==='Enter' && !e.shiftKey calls e.preventDefault() + handleSend(). Shift+Enter falls through to default textarea behavior (newline insertion). handleSend() checks content.trim() and returns early if empty—no API call for whitespace-only input. Correct and complete implementation.

isDisabled = disabled || isStreaming. Both textarea and button have disabled={isDisabled} attribute set. Textarea cursor:'not-allowed' and color:'#475569' when disabled. Button background:'#1e293b' and cursor:'not-allowed'. Container opacity:0.7. handleSend() returns early if isDisabled—no double-API call possible. Once streaming completes (finally block in startStream), isStreaming=false restores input.

index.html loads Inter (400/500/600/700) and JetBrains Mono from Google Fonts. globals.css sets font-family:'Inter', -apple-system, ... on html/body/#root. CSS variable --text-primary:#f1f5f9 set and used in message content. --text-secondary:#94a3b8 set; timestamps in Sidebar use hardcoded '#94a3b8' (consistent). Color variables are properly defined and referenced throughout.

overallSummary: The sprint is of high quality overall—routing, layout, skeleton loaders, markdown rendering, syntax highlighting, streaming input controls, and delete flow are all well-implemented. However, the sprint FAILS on one criterion: sidebar_conversation_list_populated (5/10). There is a confirmed API bug where the GET /api/conversations endpoint never returns the 'preview' field. The repository's list_conversations() SQL query includes a correlated subquery aliased as 'preview', but dict(aiosqlite.Row) does not surface computed/aliased columns, leaving the 'preview' key absent from all API responses. As a result, the sidebar never displays preview text for any conversation, even those with message history. This directly violates the sprint contract requirement for truncated preview strings. Fix: change the dict(r) conversion in list_conversations() to explicitly extract the preview column, e.g., using {k: r[k] for k in r.keys()} or cursor.description to build the dict.
